signals.headline: US Regulators Confirmed SOL and ETH as Commodities in March 2026 | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
US Regulators Confirmed SOL and ETH as Commodities in March 2026 | YES | HIGH confidence**  
This classification eliminates major legal uncertainty for allocators, following spot ETF approvals (ETH in 2024, SOL in Oct 2025), enabling clearer institutional flows amid April price gains: SOL +6.3% to $86.55, ETH +8.6% to $2,377.[1]

signals.headline: Core inflation (excluding volatile food and energy) came in at 2
signals.raw: Core inflation (excluding volatile food and energy) came in at 2.6% year-over-year**, which is notably elevated and likely to make the Federal Reserve cautious about further interest rate cuts.[5] Monthly core inflation showed only 0.2% gains, slightly better than expected, but the persistence of core price pressures alongside headline inflation volatility creates uncertainty for monetary policy.[

signals.headline: Solana Validators Approved Alpenglow Upgrade Last Week | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Solana Validators Approved Alpenglow Upgrade Last Week | YES | HIGH confidence**  
Alpenglow reworks consensus to cut finality from 12s to <200ms, positioning SOL for high-frequency trading and real-time payments; it boosts network records like 167M monthly token holders and 148M daily non-vote txns as of Apr 14.[1][3]

signals.headline: Ethereum Transactions Surged 41% Weekly | NO | MED confidence**
signals.raw:
Ethereum Transactions Surged 41% Weekly | NO | MED confidence**  
No new confirmation or announcement; surge ties to L2 fee rebates (Base, Arbitrum) and tokenized treasuries, reclaiming activity from SOL but not a resolved prediction event.[1]

signals.headline: US-Iran War Drives Inflation to 3
signals.raw:
US-Iran War Drives Inflation to 3.3% in March, Highest in Nearly 2 Years | YES | HIGH confidence**

The most significant recent development is the confirmation that **headline inflation surged to 3.3% year-over-year in March 2026**, representing the largest annual increase in nearly two years.[1][5] This marks a sharp acceleration from February's 2.4%, driven primarily by energy prices spiking 10.
